examples: Take the plough and the forge and the mallet and the lute
The word I'm thinking of is the Latin ancestor of “mallet” and it shows up in other words too.
John Mallet, sometimes called the Pope of porcelain, wrote the last article in the Transactions series.
Men may only use their right hand to swing the mallet, while women may use both hands.
The UK's oldest working prison, Shepton Mallet, in Somerset, is also among those to be shut.
